senses.definition: an extinct genus of primates from the Miocene found in Asia, believed to be related to the ancestors of modern orangutans
examples: Fossils of Sivapithecus have helped scientists understand primate evolution.
Some researchers view Sivapithecus as a close relative of the orangutan lineage.

senses.definition: a large, extinct species of hoofed mammal from the Miocene epoch, often associated with the Sivatheriinae subfamily
examples: Sivathere remains have been found in fossil beds dated to the Miocene.
The Sivathere is compared to giraffid relatives in some studies.

senses.definition: a family of extinct, large, prehistoric ruminant mammals related to the sivatheres
examples: Sivatheriidae includes several giant fossil species.
Paleontologists study the Sivatheriidae to understand horned herbivores of the Miocene.

senses.definition: a subfamily within Sivatheriidae comprising large prehistoric ruminants
examples: The Sivatheriinae subfamily is known for its enormous ossified horns.
Fossils from the Sivatheriinae reveal insights into Miocene megafauna.

senses.definition: a member of the Sivatheriinae or a form resembling sivatheres; an imitation or likeness of sivathers
examples: The skull shows sivatherioid features common among large Miocene ruminants.
Researchers described the specimen as sivatherioid in its horn arrangement.

senses.definition: a genus of extinct large-headed, horned ruminants related to sivatheriids
examples: Sivatherium fossils indicate a massive animal with heavy horns.
The Sivatherium species lived during the Miocene in Asia and Africa.

senses.definition: an archaic or dialectal form used in some contexts to mean 'silver' or 'a person named Siver' in rare proper nouns; not a standard everyday term
examples: In old texts, siver can appear as a variant of 'silver'.
The name Siver appears in local records.

senses.definition: plural form of siver; may refer to multiple people named Siver or to alloys or nicknames in dialectal usage
examples: Several locals bore the surname Sivers.
In some dialects, sivers can refer to shiny metals.

senses.definition: an uncommon or dialectal plural form; may refer to small items or people named Sivven in certain dialects
examples: The old ledger lists several sivvens as fees.
Dialects vary; sivvens might mean small coins in some regions.

senses.definition: a term for a dry, heat-damaged coconut product or a type of resin in certain contexts; also a place name in some languages
examples: The market sold siwan as a spice substitute.
Some coastal communities use siwan in traditional crafts.

senses.definition: an informal term for a Native American individual or an Indigenous person, often considered outdated; also used as a place name or surname
examples: Siwash Bay is a coastal location.
The term siwash appears in older texts and maps.

senses.definition: to wash or treat with siwash; typically not a standard modern verb; may appear in historical or dialect contexts
examples: The artifact was siwashed to remove surface rust.
Historical texts mention siwashed stones.

senses.definition: plural of siwash; refers to multiple items or instances of siwash-related items or terms
examples: The collection includes several siwashes.
In maps, several siwashes are marked by symbols.

senses.definition: the act of washing or treating with siwash materials; used in historical contexts
examples: The vessel was siwashing before display.
Ancient texts mention siwashing techniques.

senses.definition: a rare given name or surname; possibly a variant spelling of Sven/Sévèn in some languages
examples: The genealogist tracked the surname Siwen.
Siwen appears in limited historical records.

senses.definition: the number 6; a symbol for six in counting
examples: I have six apples.
Six people came to the party.

senses.definition: denoting a quantity of six
examples: She wore a six-inch heel.
We walked six miles.

senses.definition: to cut or divide into six equal parts; to produce six parts
examples: The cake was sixed into equal pieces.
They sixed the log to make six boards.

senses.definition: an archaic or dialectal term for a group of six, or a stanza of six lines in poetry
examples: A sixain of lines formed the verse.
Historical poetry sometimes used sixains.

senses.definition: alternate spelling of sixain; a six-line stanza or a group of six
examples: The poem ends with a sixaine.
In older poetry, a sixaine is a small stanza.

senses.definition: plural of sixaine; multiple six-line stanzas or groups of six
examples: The sonnet cycle contains several sixaines.
Sixaines are a feature of some medieval verses.

senses.definition: informal plural for players on a team who wear the number six; or a nickname for a unit that achieves six wins or points
examples: The Sixers beat their rivals in a close game.
The basketball team has two sixers in their roster.

senses.definition: plural of sixer; devices or items that are grouped in sixes
examples: The factory packs the screws in sixers.
Sixers of chocolates were placed in the gift box.

senses.definition: the plural of six; a group of six items or units
examples: He rolled two sixes on the dice.
The pile contained several sixes of coins.

senses.definition: an extinct sail or a type of plant morphology term; not common in modern usage
examples: The term sixfoil appears in some botanical glossaries.

senses.definition: consisting of six parts or multiplied by six
examples: The sixfold increase surprised the analysts.
They achieved a sixfold return on their investment.
